Modify Loop Conditions
The loop conditions in the toStringHelper function should be modified to correctly traverse the prototype chain and include all properties in the string representation. This can be done by changing the loop condition to only stop when the current object type is null, rather than when it is a native object type. Additionally, the function should keep track of the number of properties added to the string representation and stop adding properties when the maximum number is reached.

Handle Maximum Properties
The function should handle the case when the object has more properties than the maximum allowed for pretty printing. This can be done by adding an ellipsis to the string representation when the maximum number of properties is reached. The function should also keep track of the number of properties that are not included in the string representation and add a message indicating how many properties are not shown.

Adjust Property Addition and Ellipsis
The way properties are added to the string representation should be adjusted to ensure that all properties are included and the string representation is correctly generated. When the maximum number of properties is reached, the function should add an ellipsis to the string representation, followed by the number of properties that are not shown. For example, if the maximum number of properties is 5 and the object has 10 properties, the string representation could end with "... (5 more properties)". This would provide a clear indication of how many properties are not shown in the string representation.
